Class TestClickCombination

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Thread
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.TestClickCombination
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable
public class TestClickCombination extends Thread
An optimized worker thread that tests potential puzzle solutions from a shared work queue.

This class implements the "consumer" role in the solver's producer-consumer architecture. Each instance, referred to as a "monkey," runs on a dedicated thread. Its primary responsibility is to fetch WorkBatch objects from the CombinationQueueArray, iterate through the WorkBatch.WorkItems within, and test the described combination ranges against a local Grid clone.

The name "monkey" is a playful nod to the "infinite monkey theorem." In our context, the monkeys are tirelessly testing combinations on the grid, hoping to find the one that solves the puzzle. This terminology is used throughout the codebase to add a bit of whimsy and distinguish these worker threads from the "generator" threads.

Execution Model

Monkeys operate in a simple, continuous loop. They do not generate their own work; instead, they pull batches from a queue system. Each monkey has a preferred queue but will steal work from other queues if its own is empty.

For each WorkItem, the monkey now performs a hyper-optimized check. It computes the parity mask for the prefix once using buildParityMask(short[]), then iterates through the range of final clicks, performing a cheap satisfiesOddAdjacency(long, short) check for each one. This avoids redundant calculations and expensive grid state manipulations. If a full combination passes this check, it is applied to the grid. If it solves the puzzle, the monkey logs the solution, signals a global shutdown, and terminates.

Resource Management and Configuration

To eliminate contention, each monkey operates on its own private Grid instance, cloned from the base grid in StartYourMonkeys.GlobalConfig. All derived configuration data, such as the MASKS and EXPECTED value, are now cached as static final fields at class load time by pulling them from GlobalConfig. This allows the JIT compiler to perform powerful constant-folding optimizations in the critical validation loop.

The only shared, mutable resource monkeys interact with directly is the central CombinationQueueArray, from which they fetch work and to which they recycle WorkBatch objects after use. This design ensures that no objects are allocated in the hot path, with the minor exception of logging.

Performance and Critical Paths

The performance of the entire solver is heavily influenced by the efficiency of the monkey's main run loop and, most importantly, the satisfiesOddAdjacency(long, short) check. These sections are heavily optimized to be JIT-friendly, minimizing branching and using bitwise operations for fast validation.

Since:
2025.04 - Multi-threaded Solver Introduction
Performance Characteristics:
The main loop's overall time complexity is O(CombinationGeneratorTask.BATCH_SIZE) per batch, dominated by the O(combination.length) odd adjacency check performed for each combination.
Threading Model:
Each monkey is a Thread that operates on its own Grid instance. Work is obtained in a thread-safe manner from the shared CombinationQueueArray.
Algorithm Details:
Pulls a WorkBatch (from its own queue or by stealing), then iterates through its combinations. Each is validated with an odd adjacency check. Valid combinations are tested on the grid. On success, the monkey triggers a global shutdown.
Memory Management:
Fixed memory footprint with minimal allocations, except for logging.

  • Field Details

    • logger

      private static final Logger logger
      The logger for this class.

      Logging is used to report significant events, such as finding a solution, interruptions, and periodic progress updates. To minimize performance impact on the worker threads, an asynchronous Log4j2 logger is used, which offloads I/O operations to a separate background thread.

      Since:
      2025.05 - Async Logging Introduction
      See Also:
      • run()
      • CombinationMessage
      • Logger.debug(String)
      • Logger.info(String, Object)
      • LogManager.getLogger()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for logger retrieval.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe per Log4j2 design.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes for the static reference.

    • LOG_EVERY_N_FAILURES

      private static final int LOG_EVERY_N_FAILURES
      A constant defining the frequency of logging for failed attempts.

      To avoid overwhelming the logs and impacting performance, a debug entry for a failed combination is made only once per this many failures. This check applies only to combinations that have already passed the satisfiesOddAdjacency(long, short) check.

      Since:
      2025.05 - Logging Threshold Introduction
      See Also:
      • run()
      • CombinationMessage
      • Constant Field Values
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) retrieval for checks.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as a static final constant.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as a primitive int.

    • combinationQueue

      private final CombinationQueue combinationQueue
      The monkey's preferred CombinationQueue.

      Each monkey has an assigned queue to pull work from. This affinity helps reduce contention. If this queue is empty, the monkey will attempt to steal work from other queues in the shared queueArray.

      Since:
      2025.05 - Dedicated Queue per Monkey
      See Also:
      • TestClickCombination(String, CombinationQueue)
      • allQueuesEmpty()
      • getWork()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) dequeue operations.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe through JCTools wizardry.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes for the reference.

    • queueArray

      private static final CombinationQueueArray queueArray
      A reference to the shared CombinationQueueArray.

      This provides access to all work queues for stealing, the shared WorkBatch pool for recycling, and global state flags like CombinationQueueArray.solutionFound and CombinationQueueArray.generationComplete.

      Since:
      2025.05 - Dedicated Queue per Monkey
      See Also:
      • TestClickCombination(String, CombinationQueue)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access to queues and flags.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe through use of volatile flags and concurrent queues.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes for the reference.

    • puzzleGrid

      private final Grid puzzleGrid
      The monkey's dedicated Grid instance for testing combinations.

      To prevent any thread contention, each monkey operates on a private clone of the puzzle grid. The grid's state is reset after each failed combination test.

      Since:
      2025.04 - Multi-threaded Solver Introduction
      See Also:
      • TestClickCombination(String, CombinationQueue)
      • run()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) state resets and cell toggles.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe by design, as each thread has its own instance.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes for the reference.

    • MASKS

      private static final long[] MASKS
      A static final cache of StartYourMonkeys.GlobalConfig.CLICK_TO_TRUE_CELL_MASK.

      By caching this as a static final field at class load time, we enable the JIT compiler to perform constant-folding and other aggressive optimizations in the hot path method satisfiesOddAdjacency(long, short).

      Since:
      2025.12 - GlobalConfig Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) array access in the hot path.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as a static final constant.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes for the reference.

    • EXPECTED

      private static final long EXPECTED
      A static final cache of StartYourMonkeys.GlobalConfig.EXPECTED_MASK.

      Caching this as a static final constant allows the JIT compiler to treat it as a compile-time constant in the hot path method satisfiesOddAdjacency(long, short), leading to significant performance improvements.

      Since:
      2025.12 - GlobalConfig Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access in the hot path.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as a static final constant.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 8 bytes for the primitive long.

  • Constructor Details

    • TestClickCombination

      public TestClickCombination(String name, CombinationQueue queue)
      Constructs a monkey thread.

      The constructor is now simplified, requiring only a name and its preferred queue. All other configuration, including the puzzleGrid to clone and the shared queueArray instance, is retrieved directly from the central StartYourMonkeys.GlobalConfig.

      Parameters:
      name - The unique name for this monkey thread, used for logging.
      queue - The monkey's preferred work queue.
      Since:
      2025.04 - Monkey Thread Introduction
      See Also:
      • StartYourMonkeys.GlobalConfig.getBaseGrid()
      • CombinationQueueArray.getInstance()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) assignments plus the cost of cloning the grid.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe by nature of construction.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a new Grid clone for this thread.

  • Method Details

    • run

      public void run()
      The main execution loop for the monkey thread.

      This loop continuously fetches and processes WorkBatch objects until a solution is found or all work is complete.

      Algorithm

      The logic has been updated to process WorkBatch.WorkItem ranges instead of individual combinations, significantly improving efficiency.

      1. Attempt to get a work batch.
      2. If no work is found, check for termination conditions and sleep briefly before retrying.
      3. If a batch is acquired, iterate through each WorkBatch.WorkItem in it.
      4. For each WorkItem, compute the parity mask for its prefix once using buildParityMask(short[]).
      5. Iterate through the range of final clicks defined by the WorkItem.
      6. For each potential full combination, perform the hyper-efficient satisfiesOddAdjacency(long, short) check using the pre-computed prefix mask.
      7. If the check passes, apply the full combination to the local puzzleGrid.
      8. If the grid is solved, log the solution, trigger a global shutdown, and terminate.
      9. If not solved, reset the grid and continue to the next combination.
      10. After the batch is exhausted, recycle it to the shared pool and repeat the loop.

      Performance & Future Optimizations

      The loop is structured to be JIT-friendly. A key future optimization would be a more direct cancellation mechanism. Instead of polling the solutionFound flag, a direct interrupt or signal to all worker threads would be more efficient. Additionally, the logging of failed combinations currently requires cloning an array and creating a CombinationMessage, which could be optimized to be allocation-free.

      Specified by:
      run in interface Runnable
      Overrides:
      run in class Thread
      Since:
      2025.04 - Monkey Thread Introduction
      Performance Characteristics:
      Roughly O(WorkBatch.BATCH_SIZE * (prefixLength + finalClicks.length)) per batch. The innermost check is a highly-efficient O(1) operation.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; independent state per thread, shared access to concurrent structures.
      Algorithm Details:
      Continuously pulls batches, iterates through WorkBatch.WorkItem ranges, performs optimized satisfiesOddAdjacency(long, short) checks, tests valid combinations on the grid, and handles success or recycling.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate in the hot path, except for logging.

    • handleSuccess

      private void handleSuccess(short[] prefix, int prefixLength, short finalClick)
      Handles the successful discovery of a solution, logging it and triggering a shutdown. By extracting this logic from the run() method, we improve JIT optimization opportunities in the hot loop.
      Parameters:
      prefix - The combination prefix leading up to the final click.
      prefixLength - The length of the prefix.
      finalClick - The final click that completes the solution.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Success Handling Extraction
      See Also:
      • CombinationQueueArray.solutionFound(String, short[])
      • System.arraycopy(Object, int, Object, int, int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(prefixLength) for array copy.

    • triggerGeneratorShutdown

      private void triggerGeneratorShutdown()
      Triggers an immediate shutdown of the generator ForkJoinPool.

      When a solution is found, this method is called to halt the creation of new work. It directly invokes ForkJoinPool.shutdownNow() on the static pool instance held by CombinationGeneratorTask. This is more efficient than relying on cancellation flags, as it uses the ForkJoinPool's built-in, low-overhead mechanism to interrupt all active generator tasks.

      Since:
      2025.07.23 - Explicit Cancellation Checks Removal
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) shutdown signaling.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as per ForkJoinPool design.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate (except for logging).

    • getWork

      private WorkBatch getWork()
      Obtains a WorkBatch to process, using a work-stealing strategy.

      This method first attempts to poll from the monkey's preferred queue. If that is empty, it iterates through all other queues in the shared CombinationQueueArray to "steal" a batch. This non-blocking approach allows idle monkeys to pick up work from busy threads, helping to balance the load.

      A more advanced implementation might use some load-balanced algorithm for stealing to reduce contention, but the current linear scan is simple and effective enough.

      Returns:
      A WorkBatch to process, or null if no work is available anywhere.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Enqueueing Work Batches
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) to steal from the preferred queue, and O(queues.length) in the worst case for stealing.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; uses non-blocking queue operations.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • allQueuesEmpty

      private boolean allQueuesEmpty()
      Checks if all work queues in the system are empty.

      This is a key part of the shutdown logic. A monkey can only safely terminate when generation is complete and all queues are empty (or if the solution is found), ensuring no work is left unprocessed. This check is only performed when a monkey is idle, so its O(queues.length) complexity is acceptable.

      Returns:
      true if all queues are empty, false otherwise.
      Since:
      2025.08 - Preallocate WorkBatches for Queues
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(queues.length) in the worst case due to iteration.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; calls a thread-safe method on each queue.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • buildParityMask

      private final long buildParityMask(short[] combination)
      Computes the "parity mask" for a given combination prefix.

      This method is a critical part of the "odd adjacency" optimization. It calculates the cumulative effect of all clicks in the combination prefix by XORing their respective bitmasks from the MASKS lookup table. The resulting long is a bitmask where the Nth bit is 1 if the Nth true cell was toggled an odd number of times by the prefix, and 0 otherwise.

      This pre-computed prefix mask is then used by satisfiesOddAdjacency(long, short) to perform a final, O(1) check for each potential final click, avoiding a full re-computation for every complete combination.

      Performance & JIT Optimizations

      The method is heavily optimized for the JIT compiler. It uses local final variables to cache array references and loop bounds, which encourages the JIT to perform optimizations like loop unrolling. The core logic is a tight loop of XOR operations, which is extremely fast on modern CPUs.

      Parameters:
      combination - The combination prefix to build the mask for.
      Returns:
      The calculated parity mask as a long.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(combination.length) iteration, dominated by XOR operations.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; uses only static final data and local variables.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • satisfiesOddAdjacency

      private boolean satisfiesOddAdjacency(long prefixMask, short finalClick)
      An optimized version of the odd adjacency check that uses a pre-computed prefix mask.

      This is the most performance-critical method in the monkey's hot loop. It validates a full combination by taking the pre-computed XOR sum of the prefix (prefixMask) and XORing it with the mask for the finalClick. If the result equals the EXPECTED, it means every true cell was toggled an odd number of times, and the combination is valid.

      Parameters:
      prefixMask - The pre-computed XOR sum of the combination's prefix.
      finalClick - The final click to be tested.
      Returns:
      true if the full combination is valid, false otherwise.
      Since:
      2025.11 - Range-Based WorkItem Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) array access and bitwise operations.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe; uses only static data.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
